Aetna Better Health MD — Prior Authorization Process
▼
Submission Platform
Availity
Initiate PA and attach recent clinicals
Questionnaire Fax
844-835-4930
Fax completed questionnaire + supporting records
🧠 CA Neuropsychological Testing CPT Codes: For Aetna Better Health MD prior authorization requests involving cognitive/neuropsych testing, the two applicable CPT codes are 96132 (neuropsychological testing evaluation, first hour) and 96138 (neuropsychological testing evaluation, each additional 30 min — technician). Ensure both codes are included in the PA request when applicable.
Submission Process
PA Submission via Availity
  • Log in to Availity and navigate to Patient Registration → Auth/Referrals
  • Select Aetna Better Health as the payer
  • Enter diagnosis codes, CPT codes, ordering provider information
  • Attach patient's most recent clinical documentation
  • Submit and record the authorization reference number
Questionnaire Process
  • Review the patient's most recent office visit notes
  • Extract the relevant clinical information required by Aetna Better Health
  • Complete the questionnaire thoroughly — include dates of prior treatments and outcomes
  • Attach completed questionnaire + supporting medical records
  • Fax to: 844-835-4930
📞
Other Payors — Eligibility Verification & PA Process
▼
⚠ No Portal / No Designated Submission Method: For insurance plans that do not have a designated portal or prior authorization submission method, contact the insurance company directly to verify benefits and obtain PA submission instructions.
Call-Based Process
What to Verify on the Call
  • Patient eligibility and coverage status on the date of service
  • Whether prior authorization is required for the requested service or procedure
  • Deductible, copay, coinsurance amounts applicable
  • In-network vs. out-of-network benefit structure
  • Coordination of benefits if the patient has secondary insurance
If PA is Required
  • Obtain the appropriate submission instructions (form, fax number, portal, or reference number)
  • Ask for the clinical review department or utilization management fax number
  • Request a reference number for the call and document it in the chart
  • Ask for the expected turnaround time
  • Proceed with the authorization process per the instructions provided
Documentation Standard
For every eligibility or PA call, document in the patient chart: date of call, representative name (if provided), reference number, benefits verified, whether PA is required, submission method, expected turnaround time, and any other relevant information obtained.
⏰
Prior Authorization Extension — Rules & Process
▼
⚠ CRITICAL RULE: A prior authorization (PA) can only be extended once. The extension request must be submitted close to the expiration date of the current authorization — do not wait until after it expires.
Extension vs. Reinitiation
✅ Extension (Before Expiration)
  • Call the insurance contact number and ask to be transferred to the Utilization Management / Utilization Department
  • Say: "Please transfer my call to the utilization department for extension purposes"
  • Provide the current authorization number and request an extension
  • Extension period: 60 days (some insurances allow 30 days only — confirm on the call)
  • Record the new authorization period and document in the chart
❌ After Expiration — Full Reinitiation Required
  • If the PA has already expired, an extension is no longer possible
  • You must reinitiate the prior authorization from scratch
  • The insurance will send a new questionnaire and additional information requests
  • The entire PA process repeats — all steps must be completed again
  • Turnaround time: 14 business days (up to) from reinitiation
Extension Rules by Insurance
Extension Period
  • Most insurances: 60-day extension
  • Some insurances: 30-day extension only
  • Always confirm extension duration with the utilization representative
After the Extension Expires
  • No further extensions are permitted
  • Must reinitiate a new PA request
  • Questionnaire and clinical review will be required again
What to Say on the Call
  • Call the payer's provider services line
  • Request transfer to the Utilization Department
  • Provide: auth number, patient info, reason for extension
  • Document the representative name, new auth period, and reference number

🧠 CA Program Standards — Policies, Documentation & Quality

Scheduling guidelines, documentation requirements, common errors, and quality indicators for the Cognitive Assessment program.

Key Principle

Cognitive Assessment is an important diagnostic and monitoring tool. Every eligible patient should receive the assessment at the appropriate interval, with complete documentation, proper prior authorization, and accurate tracking to ensure continuity of care.

📌
CA Program Policies Required
▼
  • Baseline assessment: Perform a baseline Cognitive Assessment for all newly diagnosed patients with memory impairment, unless the provider determines it is clinically unnecessary and documents that decision.
  • Annual follow-up: Repeat the CA annually for patients with stable memory impairment or a normal baseline result.
  • Every 6 months: Repeat the CA every 6 months for patients with documented cognitive impairment or when the provider specifically requests more frequent monitoring.
  • Concussion patients: Always initiate prior authorization when required by the patient's insurance, as Cognitive Assessment is considered part of the concussion evaluation protocol.
  • Always create a CA encounter in TalkEHR when testing is performed. A completed assessment with no encounter is not billable and creates a documentation gap.
  • Do not start the assessment before the provider has seen the patient, unless the provider has specifically instructed otherwise for that visit workflow.
  • Non-covered insurance: Do not perform Cognitive Assessments for patients whose insurance does not cover the service (see Insurance Coverage Guidelines above) unless specifically approved by the practice.
📄
CA Documentation Requirements
▼

Every completed Cognitive Assessment encounter must include all of the following before it is considered complete:

  • ✔ Provider order or recommendation documented in the chart
  • ✔ Prior authorization status recorded — approved auth number on file if required
  • ✔ CA encounter created in TalkEHR for the date of service
  • ✔ Test results documented accurately
  • ✔ Interpretation of results (normal, mild impairment, significant impairment, etc.)
  • ✔ Follow-up plan documented — when the next assessment is scheduled or recommended
  • ✔ Results uploaded to OpenEvidence
🚫A CA encounter without a provider order, missing results, or no follow-up plan is incomplete. Do not mark the encounter closed until every item above is confirmed.
🚫
CA — Common Errors to Avoid
▼
  • Performing a CA without a provider recommendation — testing must be ordered or recommended by the provider first
  • Forgetting to obtain prior authorization — especially for concussion patients and plans that require it; a missing PA results in a claim denial
  • Completing the assessment without creating a CA encounter in TalkEHR — unbillable and undocumented
  • Failing to schedule annual or 6-month follow-up testing — tracking repeat assessments is part of closing the loop on every CA
  • Starting the assessment before confirming provider workflow — if the patient has not yet been seen by the provider, hold the CA unless specifically instructed otherwise
  • Not uploading results to OpenEvidence — results must be accessible across the care team
✅
CA Daily Checklist
▼
Progress:
0 / 6
  • ✓
    Reviewed today's patients scheduled for Cognitive Assessment — provider recommendation confirmed for each
  • ✓
    Prior authorization verified as approved (where required) before testing begins
  • ✓
    CA encounter created in TalkEHR for each patient who completed testing today
  • ✓
    Test results documented and abnormal results routed to provider same day
  • ✓
    Results uploaded to OpenEvidence
  • ✓
    Next assessment scheduled or recommended follow-up interval documented in chart
📊
CA Quality Indicators (KPIs)
▼
Prior Authorization Completion Rate
% of CAs requiring PA where authorization was obtained before testing
Documentation Accuracy
% of CA encounters with all required elements complete at time of closure
CA Encounter Completion Rate
% of completed assessments that have a corresponding CA encounter created in TalkEHR
Annual Follow-Up Compliance
% of eligible patients who received their annual repeat CA within the recommended window
6-Month Follow-Up Compliance
% of cognitively impaired patients who received their 6-month repeat CA on schedule
Provider Correction Rate
% of CA encounters requiring correction after provider review — reflects upstream documentation accuracy
🎓
Cognitive Assessment Knowledge Quiz Required — All Staff
▼
📋

Cognitive Assessment Knowledge Check

10 questions  ·  80% required to pass  ·  Certificate awarded on successful completion

💊 Medication Assistance Programs

Manufacturer patient assistance programs (PAP), copay cards, Maryland state programs, and federal assistance resources to help eligible patients obtain prescribed medications at low or no cost.

PAPCopay CardsLilly CaresBiogen CaresAbbVieAmgenMPAPExtra HelpNeedyMedsPAN FoundationMaryland 211
Key Principle

Completing a medication assistance form is only the beginning of the process. Every application should be tracked until the patient receives a final decision and, if approved, begins therapy. Clear documentation, timely follow-up, and patient education help ensure that financial barriers do not prevent patients from receiving needed medications.

📌
Program Policies & Tracking Requirements Required
▼
Core Policies
  • Use the correct manufacturer form for each medication — verify the manufacturer before starting any application (e.g., Emgality → Lilly; Ubrelvy → AbbVie)
  • Every application must be entered into the Medication Assistance Tracking Sheet immediately upon submission
  • Patients may submit their own applications after receiving instructions and the correct forms — this saves staff time and allows patients to follow up directly with the manufacturer
  • Maintain commonly used assistance forms in stock and provide clear instructions to patients
Tracking Sheet — Required Fields
  • Patient name & date of birth
  • Medication name & manufacturer
  • Date form given to patient
  • Date form returned by patient
  • Date scanned into chart
  • Assigned MA
  • Date submitted to manufacturer
  • Submission method (fax / portal / mail)
  • Current status
  • Approval / denial / outcome
Front Desk Responsibilities — Returned Forms
  • Date-stamp the form when it is returned by the patient
  • Scan the completed form into the patient's chart
  • Enter the return date into the tracking sheet
  • Route the form to the assigned MA immediately — do not leave forms on a desk without documentation
🎓
Patient Education — What Patients Need to Know
▼

Before a patient takes home an assistance application or is directed to apply online, they must understand each of the following:

  • What documents are required — proof of income (tax return, pay stubs, or SSA letter), insurance card, valid ID, and completed provider section
  • How to submit the application — online portal, fax, or mail depending on the manufacturer
  • How to contact the manufacturer directly if they have questions during the process
  • How to follow up on their application status — most manufacturers have a toll-free patient services line
  • When to notify the office of approval or denial — especially if approved, so we can coordinate prescription release and delivery
  • Expected timeline — most programs take 2–6 weeks to process; some have expedited options

🏭 Manufacturer Patient Assistance Programs (PAP)

Free or reduced-cost medication programs offered directly by drug manufacturers for uninsured or underinsured patients. Income eligibility thresholds vary by program.

💉
Neurology — Manufacturer PAP Quick Reference
▼
MedicationManufacturer / ProgramContact / ApplyNotes
Emgality
(galcanezumab)		Eli Lilly

LillyCares.com		Free drug for uninsured/underinsured. Income ≤400% FPL. Also has copay card for insured patients (Emgality Savings Card).
Kisunla
(donanemab)		Eli Lilly
Lilly Cares Foundation		1-800-545-5979
LillyCares.com		Free drug program for eligible patients. Lilly also offers the Kisunla Savings Program for commercially insured.

(ubrogepant)		AbbVie

myAbbVieAssist.com		Free medication for uninsured/underinsured patients meeting income criteria. AbbVie also offers a copay card for insured patients.

(onabotulinumtoxinA)		AbbVie (Allergan)

myAbbVieAssist.com		PAP for uninsured. Allergan Access program for commercial insured. Requires documentation of chronic migraine (≥15 headache days/month).

(erenumab)		Amgen

(ocrelizumab)		Genentech

Tysabri
(natalizumab)		Biogen
Biogen Cares™		1-800-456-2255
BiogenCares.com		Free medication for uninsured/underinsured. Must be enrolled in TOUCH® prescribing program. Biogen financial assistance team can also help with commercial copays.
Tecfidera / Vumerity
(dimethyl fumarate / diroximel fumarate)		Biogen
Biogen Cares™		1-800-456-2255
BiogenCares.com		Free medication for eligible uninsured patients. Copay assistance for commercially insured. Income-based eligibility.
Aubagio / Lemtrada
(teriflunomide / alemtuzumab)		Sanofi Genzyme
MS One to One™		1-855-676-6326
MSOnetOne.com		Personalized support including financial assistance for eligible patients. Copay program available.
Kesimpta / Gilenya / Mayzent
(ofatumumab / fingolimod / siponimod)		Novartis
Novartis Patient Assistance Program		1-800-277-2254

Leqembi
(lecanemab)		Eisai / Biogen
LEQEMBI Patient Support		1-888-LEQEMBI
LEQEMBI.com		Patient support program for Alzheimer's treatment. Financial assistance available for eligible patients. Requires documented amyloid confirmation and enrollment.
💳
Copay Assistance Cards — Commercially Insured Patients
▼
⚠️Copay cards are generally NOT valid for Medicare, Medicaid, or other government-funded insurance. Always confirm the patient's insurance type before enrolling them in a copay card program. Submitting a government-insured patient's claim with a copay card can constitute fraud.
How Copay Cards Work
  • Manufacturer provides a card (physical or digital) that covers part or all of the patient's copay at the pharmacy
  • Most are valid only for commercially insured patients (private insurance, employer-sponsored plans)
  • Cards are typically free to enroll and activate online or by phone
  • Most have an annual maximum benefit (e.g., $12,000/year)
  • The card does not replace insurance — it supplements the patient's cost share after the claim is processed
Enrollment Process
  • Enroll online at the manufacturer's website or call the patient support line
  • Most programs require: patient name, DOB, insurance info, prescribing provider NPI, and the prescribed medication
  • A card number or BIN/PCN/Group code will be provided — give this to the pharmacy with the prescription
  • Document copay card enrollment and the card details in the patient's chart

🏛 Maryland State & Federal Assistance Programs

State-funded, federally administered, and nonprofit programs available to Maryland residents to help with prescription drug costs.

🦀
Maryland-Specific Programs
▼
MPAP — Maryland Pharmacy Assistance Program
  • Who qualifies: Maryland residents age 65+ enrolled in Medicare Part D, or adults with a disability who have Medicare; income at or below 300% FPL
  • What it covers: Helps pay Medicare Part D premiums and cost-sharing (copays/deductibles) for prescription drugs
  • How to apply: Call 1-800-551-6965 or visit mmcp.health.maryland.gov
  • Applications submitted through the Maryland Medical Assistance Program
Maryland Medicaid (Medical Assistance)
  • Who qualifies: Low-income Maryland residents; income thresholds vary by household size and eligibility category
  • What it covers: Comprehensive prescription drug coverage for enrolled patients
  • How to apply: Maryland Health Connection — marylandhealthconnection.gov or call 1-855-642-8572
  • Patients with Medicaid should be directed to verify their formulary before starting a specialty drug
Maryland 211
  • What it is: Maryland's statewide helpline connecting residents to local health, social, and financial assistance resources — including prescription help
  • How to access: Dial 2-1-1 or visit 211md.org
  • Especially useful for patients who need help beyond what standard PAP programs offer
ADAP — AIDS Drug Assistance Program (Maryland)
  • Who qualifies: Maryland residents with HIV/AIDS who are uninsured or underinsured and meet income criteria
  • What it covers: HIV-related medications and some ancillary medications
  • Contact: Maryland ADAP — 410-767-6500 or mdadap.com
  • Relevant if neurological conditions are HIV-related (e.g., HIV-associated neurocognitive disorder)
🇺🇸
Federal Programs — Medicare Extra Help (Low Income Subsidy)
▼
What Is Extra Help?
  • Federal program administered by the Social Security Administration (SSA) that helps Medicare beneficiaries pay Part D prescription drug costs
  • Reduces or eliminates the Part D premium, deductible, and copayments
  • Full Extra Help: Income ≤135% FPL — minimal copays; most drugs covered at very low cost
  • Partial Extra Help: Income 136–150% FPL — reduced premiums and deductibles
How to Apply
  • Online: SSA.gov/extrahelp
  • Phone: 1-800-772-1213 (Social Security)
  • In person: Local Social Security office
  • Patients automatically qualify if enrolled in Medicaid, MSP, or SSI
  • Encourage Medicare patients who are struggling with drug costs to apply — this is one of the most impactful programs available
ℹ️Maryland MPAP and Medicare Extra Help can often be used together. A patient may qualify for both — MPAP covers the Part D premium while Extra Help reduces copays. Direct patients to both programs when applicable.

🤝 National Nonprofit & Third-Party Assistance Programs

Independent foundations and databases that provide financial assistance or help identify resources for patients regardless of insurance status.

🔍
Nonprofit Programs — Quick Reference
▼
ProgramPhone / WebsiteWhat It Does
NeedyMeds1-800-503-6897
NeedyMeds.org		Free database of PAP programs, copay assistance, disease-specific programs, and discount drug cards. Searchable by medication or diagnosis. Excellent first-stop resource for both staff and patients.
RxAssistRxAssist.orgComprehensive directory of manufacturer PAP programs with direct application links. Maintained by Volunteers in Health Care. Good for verifying current program requirements.
PAN Foundation
(Patient Access Network)		1-866-316-7263
PANFoundation.org		Provides copay assistance grants for underinsured patients with life-threatening, chronic, or rare diseases. Disease-specific funds — search by diagnosis. Grants are first-come, first-served when funds are available.
HealthWell Foundation1-800-675-8416
HealthWellFoundation.org		Financial assistance for insured patients who cannot afford their copays, premiums, or deductibles for certain conditions. Disease-specific eligibility. Neurological conditions often covered.
Patient Advocate Foundation
Co-Pay Relief		1-800-532-5274
PatientAdvocate.org		Direct financial assistance for copays, health insurance premiums, and cost-of-treatment for patients with chronic illness. Also offers case management services.
BenefitsCheckUp
(NCOA)		BenefitsCheckUp.orgFree screening tool from the National Council on Aging. Identifies federal, state, and local benefit programs a patient may qualify for — including prescription assistance, utility help, and more. Useful for elderly patients.
GoodRxGoodRx.com
GoodRx app		Discount drug card that reduces retail prices at participating pharmacies. Not insurance — but can significantly lower out-of-pocket cost for generic and some brand medications. Free to use; no enrollment required.
Partnership for Prescription Assistance (PPA)1-888-477-2669
PPARx.org		Centralized resource connecting patients to more than 475 public and private PAP programs. Single application portal for multiple programs.
🚫
Common Errors to Avoid
▼
  • Using the wrong manufacturer form — each drug has a specific program and form; using the wrong one means starting over
  • Not tracking returned paperwork — patient returns a completed form and it sits unscanned and unentered
  • Leaving forms on a desk without documentation — a form not entered into the tracking sheet is a form that will be lost
  • Not following up on incomplete applications — manufacturers frequently request additional income documentation or provider information
  • Not notifying patients of approval or denial — patient doesn't know they were approved and never picks up the medication
  • Assuming a submitted application is complete without confirmation — call or check the portal to confirm receipt and completeness
  • Using a copay card for a Medicare/Medicaid patient — this is a compliance violation; verify insurance before enrolling
✅
Medication Assistance Daily Checklist
▼
Progress:
0 / 6
  • ✓
    Reviewed new applications — all entered into tracking sheet with required fields completed
  • ✓
    Reviewed returned forms — date-stamped, scanned into chart, entered in tracker, routed to assigned MA
  • ✓
    Updated tracking sheet with current status for all pending applications
  • ✓
    Followed up on applications pending >2 weeks — contacted manufacturer for status update
  • ✓
    Responded to any manufacturer requests for additional documentation or information
  • ✓
    Notified patients of any approval, denial, or status change — documented notification in chart
📊
Quality Indicators (KPIs)
▼
Application Submission Time
Days between patient eligibility identified and application submitted to manufacturer
Approval Rate
% of submitted applications resulting in approval
Average Time — Application to Decision
Average days from submission to manufacturer approval or denial
Tracking Sheet Completion Rate
% of applications with all required tracking fields completed
Patient Notification Compliance
% of approval/denial decisions communicated to the patient and documented in chart
Medication Start Time After Approval
Days from manufacturer approval to patient receiving first dose of medication

• To initiate an order: include pt name, DOB, medication name, appt date, and office delivery address

• Rx requires up to 14 days for delivery from receipt date
• Orders delivered 5–7 business days from patient approval

🏥 Patient Portal — TalkPHR Registration Guide

Step-by-step guide for helping patients register and access the Mir Neurology Patient Portal (TalkPHR 360). Use this guide when onboarding new patients or troubleshooting portal access issues.

Patient PortalTalkPHRRegistrationAll StaffPatient Onboarding
ℹ️For Staff: When a new patient is entered into TalkEHR, send them a portal invitation from the system. Walk them through these steps if they call for help. The portal link is talkphr.com.
📹 Video Walkthrough

Full onboarding walkthrough — share this link with patients: mirneurology.com/talkphr

📋 Registration Process — Step by Step

1
Step 01
Receive Your Invitation

The patient will receive an invitation on both their email and cellphone containing a secure registration link and a PIN code.

Staff tip: If the patient says they didn't receive the invitation, verify their email and phone number in TalkEHR and resend the invite.
2
Step 02
Verify PIN & Continue

Open the registration link. The phone/email and PIN will already be pre-filled. If the PIN becomes invalid, the patient should click Re-Generate PIN and continue.

Staff tip: PINs expire after a short time. If the patient waited too long to click the link, advise them to click "Re-Generate PIN" on the screen.
3
Step 03
Create Your Password

The patient sets up a secure password using uppercase letters, numbers, and special characters. This password will be used to log in to both the web portal and the mobile app.

Staff tip: Remind patients to save their password. If they forget it later, they can use the "Forgot Password" link on the login screen.
4
Step 04
Complete Intake Forms

The patient fills out intake information including contact details, medical history, and required consent forms. This information feeds directly into TalkEHR.

Staff tip: Encourage patients to complete this before their first visit — it saves time at check-in and ensures the chart is complete before the provider sees them.
5
Step 05
Access Your Dashboard

After completing registration, the patient is redirected to their Patient Portal Dashboard where they can view appointments, messages, and health records.

6
Step 06
Download TalkPHR 360

Visit the App Store (iPhone) or Google Play (Android) and install the TalkPHR 360 – CareCloud app on the mobile device.

🍎 App Store ▶ Google Play
7
Step 07
Sign Into The App

Login using the registered email or phone number and the password created during step 3.

Staff tip: If the patient can't log in to the app, have them try logging in on the web first at talkphr.com to confirm their credentials work.
8
Step 08
You're All Set!

The Patient Portal is now active and ready to use for appointments, forms, secure messaging, and billing.

✅

Successfully Registered!

The patient now has full access to the Mir Neurology Patient Portal.

🍎 Download on App Store ▶ Get it on Google Play
🔧
Common Patient Issues & How to Help
▼
Issue: Didn't receive invitation
  • Verify email and phone number in TalkEHR
  • Check if invitation went to spam/junk folder
  • Resend invitation from TalkEHR
  • Confirm the patient's preferred contact method
Issue: PIN is invalid or expired
  • Instruct patient to click Re-Generate PIN on the registration screen
  • PINs expire quickly — patient should complete registration in one sitting
Issue: Forgot password
  • Direct patient to talkphr.com
  • Click "Forgot Password" on the login screen
  • They will receive a reset link via email or phone
Issue: Can't log in to the app
  • Have patient try web login at talkphr.com first
  • Confirm they are using the correct app: TalkPHR 360 – CareCloud
  • Confirm email/phone used during registration

🛠️ Important Support Contacts

Vendor support lines for the practice's key systems. When contacting support, always document the case number, representative name, date, and issue summary. Follow up persistently until the issue is fully resolved.

TalkEHREverywareVendor SupportIT
⚠️Follow-Up Is Required. Opening a support case is only the first step. Continue calling and following up until your issue is fully resolved. Do not assume a case will be handled without active follow-through on your end.

🖥️ TalkEHR Support

732-873-5133
Press Option 1, then enter Extension 106
Point of Contact
Ahtisham
🔁Keep following up. After opening a case, continue calling until the issue is resolved. Do not wait for them to call back.
When You Contact Support — Always Document
  • Date and time of call
  • Case or ticket number provided
  • Name of representative you spoke with
  • Summary of the issue and what was done
  • Expected resolution date or next follow-up date
Follow-Up Script"Hi, I'm calling to follow up on case [#]. The issue is [description]. It has not yet been resolved. What is the current status and when can we expect a fix?"

💬 Everyware Support

888-831-6288
Ask to speak with Mark or Karen
Points of Contact
Mark  ·  Karen
When You Contact Support — Always Document
  • Date and time of call
  • Name of representative you spoke with (Mark or Karen)
  • Summary of the issue and what was done
  • Any case or ticket number provided
  • Next follow-up date if not immediately resolved
Follow-Up Script"Hi, this is [your name] from Mir Neurology. I'm following up on an issue we reported on [date]. We haven't received a resolution yet — can you give me a status update?"
General Principle — Vendor Support

Vendor support teams handle many clients simultaneously. Your issue will not resolve itself. The responsibility is on you to open the case, document the details, and follow up consistently until the problem is closed. If you are not getting resolution, escalate to the operations manager.
